commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: "For they gat not the land in possession by their own sword, neither did their own arm save them; but Thy right hand, and Thine arm, and the light of Thy countenance" (ver. Thy "right hand" is Thy Power: Thine "arm" is Thy Son Himself. And "the light of Thy countenance. " What means this, but that Thou wert present with them, in miracles of such a sort that Thy presence was perceived. For when God's presence with us appears by any miracle, do we see His face with our own eyes. It is by the effect of the miracle He intimates to man His presence. In fact, what do all persons say, who express wonder at facts of this description. "I saw God present. " "But Thy right hand, and Thine arm, and the light of Thy countenance; because Thou pleasedst in them:" i. didst so deal with them, that Thou wert well-pleasing in them: that whoso considered how they were being dealt with, might say, that "God is with them of a truth;" and it is God that moves them. Was He then other than now He is. " Away with the supposition. For what follows.

methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
